close_reason: Implemented tiered CI smoke test suite with GitHub Actions workflow
description:
Identify and implement a smoke test suite suitable for CI pipelines.

## Goals
- Fast enough to run on every PR (~2-5 minutes max)
- Catches most regressions without running full test suite
- Tests critical paths across all supported languages

## Candidate Tests

### Nix/Flake Health
- `nix flake check --no-build` - validates flake structure
- `nix build .#turnkey-prelude` - prelude builds correctly
- `nix develop --impure -c true` - shell enters successfully

### Buck2 Integration
- Build a simple target from each language:
  - `buck2 build //examples/go-hello:go-hello`
  - `buck2 build //examples/rust-hello:rust-hello`
  - `buck2 build //examples/python-hello:python-hello`
  - `buck2 build //examples/typescript-hello:typescript-hello`
- Run one target to verify runtime works

### Dependency Cells
- Verify godeps cell builds
- Verify rustdeps cell builds  
- Verify pydeps cell builds
- Verify jsdeps cell builds

### Tool Smoke Tests
- `godeps-gen --help`
- `rustdeps-gen --help`
- `pydeps-gen --help`
- `jsdeps-gen --help`
- `tk --help`

## Implementation
- Create `scripts/ci-smoke.sh` or similar
- Add GitHub Actions workflow
- Consider matrix testing across platforms (linux, darwin)
title: Define CI smoke test suite
